rulings.Case Title: CAROL BLUMENFELD SACHAL VS. JUAN DE DIOS RODRIGUEZ-PINA BLANCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Moiton And Moiton To Reject Unapproved Listing Agent, To Revoke Appointment Neutral Partition Referee And Vacate Proposed Sale; And For An Award Of Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 4, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANT JUAN RODRIGUEZ-PINA BLANCO's Motion To Reject Unapproved Listing Agent, To Revoke Appointment Neutral Partition Referee And Vacate Proposed Sale; And For An Award Of Attorneys Fees: GRANTED with the exception of the request for attorney's fees. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE SU LING LOO VS. CHRISTINE LE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 4, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANTS CHRISTINE LE and WILLIAM LE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: GRANTED. All causes of action are barred by statute of limitations. Muktarian v. Barmby (1965) 63 Cal.2d 558. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE LOW VS. KWONG YIP CHEUNG et al

rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 4, 2014, Line 9: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories: DENIED without prejudice to additional, more narrowly focused, discovery. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY ROCCA VS. AUDREY EASHMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 4, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANTS FISHER REALTOR, INC and CLARA KATES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Sustained with leave to amend to allege/attach a contract with the moving defendants; to allege fraud with requisite specificity and to allege facts giving rise to fiduciary duty. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SOCHI TA VS. LOUIS KIRTMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 4, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT: Sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to sufficiently allege the terms of the contract and defendant's breach of those terms. The Court will not rule on the statute of limitations until the contract and breach are sufficiently alleged. = (501/REQ)
